About: It is about the High Court and Court of Appeal of England and Wales.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Inns of Court.
It is associated with St Clement Danes church.
It is associated with the Australian High Commission.
It is associated with King's College London.
It is associated with the London School of Economics.
BuiltBy: It was built by Messrs Bull & Sons of Southampton.
BuiltIn: It was built in eight years.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a large grey stone edifice.
HasA: It has a statue of Edwin Wilkins Field.
It has a tall clock tower.
It has a clock and five bells.
It has offices for courtroom staff.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by Victorian Gothic Revival style.
It is characterized by a symmetrical main frontage.
It is characterized by an arched doorway.
It is characterized by a five-part window.
It is characterized by a gable containing a rose window.
It is characterized by a sculpture of Jesus.
It is characterized by statues of Moses, Solomon and Alfred the Great.
It is characterized by towers containing lancet windows.
It is characterized by side wings.
It is characterized by a pyramidal roof.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the reformist political movement.
It was influenced by the High Victorian architectural movement.
IsA: It is one of the largest courts in Europe.
It is a Grade I listed building.
KnownAs: It is known as the Law Courts.
LocatedIn: It is located on the Strand.
It is located within the City of Westminster.
It is located near the boundary with the City of London.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2021.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Westminster Hall for centuries.
UsedAs: It is used as a "Nightingale Court" for criminal trials.
UsedFor: It is used for criminal trials.
